items.prompt: What does speaker diarization assign in audio annotation?
choices: Who spoke when in a recording
items.explain: Diarization segments audio by speaker identity across time.

items.prompt: Example 4.17 focuses on which audio label type?
items.explain: Annotators produce transcripts aligned to call audio for ASR training.

items.prompt: Activity recognition in video labels clip-level behaviors rather than requiring a box on every frame.
items.explain: Clip-level tags such as pass or foul can sit above per-frame object labels.

items.prompt: In Example 4.24, what three value bands may rows receive?
items.sample_answer: High, medium, and low value bands derived from purchase history.

items.prompt: Why is video annotation often more costly than image annotation?
choices: Objects and actions evolve across frames, combining spatial and temporal work
Tabular rows are always larger than video files
items.explain: Temporal consistency and frame-level work raise labeling effort.
